text: They opened the front door and the house felt wrong in a way they couldn't name, as if someone had been inside and rearranged the air
The MRI machine pulled her in slowly and the ceiling was six inches from her face and she could hear her own breathing amplified inside the tube like someone else was in there with her
He woke at 3 AM to the smoke detector chirping in the hallway and when he opened the bedroom door the air was thick and gray and tasted like burning plastic
The trail markers stopped appearing and the trees looked the same in every direction and the sun was behind clouds so she could not tell which way was west anymore
During the presentation his laptop froze on the first slide and three hundred people sat in their seats watching him and the screen blinked once and went black
She picked up the phone and the voice on the other end said this is Doctor Owens and I have your biopsy results and would you like to sit down
The elevator stopped between floors with a mechanical groan and the lights flickered twice and then went out and the emergency button made no sound when he pressed it
The car started hydroplaning on the overpass and the steering wheel went loose in his hands and for two seconds the vehicle was moving sideways across the lane lines
At 38,000 feet the plane dropped without warning and the overhead bins popped open and a drink cart rolled down the aisle and the flight attendant grabbed the nearest seat
The dog, who never growled, was standing stiff-legged at the sliding glass door staring into the dark backyard with its hackles up and a low vibration coming from deep in its chest
She checked the rearview mirror and the same gray sedan was still behind her, four turns in a row now, keeping exactly two car lengths back
The ice made a deep booming sound under his boots and a crack appeared that ran from his left foot toward the middle of the lake in a perfectly straight line
He reached into the mailbox and his fingers touched something that moved and he yanked his hand back so fast he scraped his knuckles on the metal flap
The building's fire alarm went off and she grabbed her daughter and headed for the stairs but the stairwell was already filling with people moving too fast in the dark
On the ultrasound screen the technician went quiet and moved the wand slowly back and forth over the same spot without saying anything for a long time
The toddler was right there a second ago, right by the bench, and now the playground was full of children and none of them was his and his eyes moved faster than his legs could
The bridge swayed under the wind and through the metal grating beneath her feet she could see the river two hundred feet below, brown and fast-moving
He opened the bank app and the balance said negative four hundred dollars and he checked the date and rent had not even come out yet
The power went out during the storm and the basement pump stopped humming and she could already hear water trickling through the foundation
She heard footsteps on the gravel outside the tent at 2 AM, too heavy and too slow to be an animal, and they circled once and stopped near the zippered door
The rope bridge had missing planks and the ones that remained were dark with rot and the guide said it was fine but did not go first
His chest tightened and his left arm went numb and he stood in the kitchen thinking very clearly that this was either stress or the other thing, the serious thing, and he could not tell which
The notification from the school said lockdown in progress, shelter in place, and her son's phone went straight to voicemail and she called it again and again standing in the office parking lot
The ladder shifted two inches to the right while he was on the top rung and the gutter he was holding crumpled like foil under his grip
The test results portal loaded slowly and she sat with her cursor hovering over the link marked View Results the way someone stands at the edge of a diving board
A shape moved in the water beneath the kayak, longer than the boat, and the surface bulged slightly and then went still
She woke up and could not move her legs and then sensation came back in pins and needles but for those four seconds the bedroom ceiling looked like the last thing she would ever see
The dentist's drill hit something and he flinched and the dentist said hmm in a tone that was not reassuring and reached for a different instrument
Walking home from the bus stop the streetlight above her went out with a tick and the next one was a full block ahead and the alley on her left was a long dark rectangle
The wave was bigger than it looked from shore and it picked him up and turned him and he could not tell which direction was up and his lungs were starting to ache
During the earthquake the bookshelves swayed and she dove under the desk and heard glass breaking in every room at once and the floor moved like something alive under her palms
The mechanic looked under the car and came out with a face that said this is going to be expensive and pointed to a part that was supposed to be attached to another part and was not
He typed his symptoms into the search bar at midnight and the results were arranged from benign to terminal and he read all of them in order and could not stop
The child's fever had not broken in six hours and the thermometer kept showing the same number and the on-call nurse said bring her in now, not in the morning, now
She realized halfway across the intersection that the light had already turned and a truck was accelerating from her left and her body moved before her mind did
The hotel room door was ajar when he came back from dinner and he was certain he had closed it and the hallway behind him was empty and very long
The pilot came on the intercom with that too-calm voice that pilots use when something is actually wrong and said they would be diverting to a different airport without explaining why
Standing at the podium she looked out at the audience and her notes blurred and the microphone picked up the sound of the paper shaking in her hands
The text from his ex said I need to tell you something about the kids and the three dots pulsed for a long time before the next message came through
The rattling sound came from inside the wall behind the headboard and it was rhythmic and deliberate and did not match anything that should be living inside a wall
The elevator shuddered between floors and the lights flickered once, twice, then went out entirely, and in the darkness the cables above made a sound like something shifting its weight
